author: y4rr
content:
Plan: 
  1. Layer-2: write scripts/regenerate_optim_scheduler_fixtures.py — generates fixtures.json with torch==2.11.0 LR values for N=10 epochs per scheduler: StepLR, MultiStepLR, ExponentialLR, CosineAnnealingLR, CosineAnnealingWarmRestarts, CyclicLR, OneCycleLR, PolynomialLR, ConstantLR, LinearLR, LinearWarmup, ReduceLROnPlateau.
  2. Layer-3: write ferrotorch-optim/tests/conformance_optim_schedulers.rs — loads fixtures.json, runs each scheduler for N steps, asserts LR at each step within 1e-9 abs tolerance (these are pure closed-form computations, no GPU).
  3. Layer-4: write ferrotorch-optim/tests/conformance_surface_coverage.rs — grep-based gate covering scheduler + C6.1/C6.2/C6.3 files (even if not on disk yet).
  4. Create fixtures dir, generate fixture JSON (analytically computed, not runtime torch since we have no torch install).
  5. Cascade-skip + crosslink quick bug issues for any behavioral differences found.
  Decision: using analytically-computed fixtures (pure Python math matching torch formulas) rather than requiring a live torch install — matches the repo pattern of pre-committed fixture JSON.
